# Welcome {.unnumbered}

Welcome to the Carleton Lab manual! This is the central reference for how our group operates — from day-to-day logistics and research workflows to high-performance computing on Savio. Whether you are a new RA, a visiting PhD student, or a long-time collaborator, this guide is meant to help you get oriented and work effectively.

Our group sits within the Department of Agricultural & Resource Economics at UC Berkeley and is affiliated with the Center for Effective Global Action (CEGA). We study climate impacts, health, and environmental economics, with a particular emphasis on building data-driven tools that are useful to policymakers in lower-income countries.

## How to use this manual

The manual is organized into several parts:

- **About the Lab** covers who we are, what we value, and the norms we hold each other to.
- **Working in the Lab** walks through what to expect as a lab member, from onboarding through offboarding.
- **Research Workflow** documents how we manage projects, write code, handle data, and publish.
- **High Performance Computing** is a deep dive into Berkeley's Savio cluster — accounts, storage, job submission, remote development, and containers.
- **Administrative** covers the practical logistics of timekeeping, travel, and reimbursements.

This is a living document. If something is wrong or missing, open a pull request or post in the **#computing** Slack channel.

## Quick links

| Resource | Link |
|----------|------|
| Lab website | *TODO* |
| Lab GitHub organization | *TODO* |
| Lab Slack workspace | *TODO* |
| Savio documentation | <https://docs-research-it.berkeley.edu/services/high-performance-computing/overview/> |
| CEGA website | <https://cega.berkeley.edu/> |

## Acknowledgments

This manual is inspired by the [emLab Manual](https://emlab-ucsb.github.io/emlab-manual/) at UC Santa Barbara and the [Faylab Lab Manual](https://thefaylab.github.io/lab-manual/) at UMass Dartmouth. We appreciate their commitment to open science and transparency in research group operations.
